articles.title: Trump says Iran's government 'seriously fractured' as he extends ceasefire

children.content: US President Donald Trump says he will hold off attacking Iran until Tehran's negotiating teams come up with a unified proposal for a peace deal.

items.title: What life is like for those trapped on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: For months seafarers have been stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, tanker captain Raman Kapoor is speaking out about the fears and uncertainty of his crew.

items.title: Government working with two companies to underwrite fertiliser imports

children.content: Australian farmers have been assured more fertiliser imports are on the way as the government is working with two companies to secure supply. Follow live.

image.altText: Julie Collins

items.title: The growing problem stopping Melbourne residents using their microwaves

children.content: A power company warns that growing electricity demand is creating an undervoltage issue as Victoria's infrastructure struggles to keep up with the transition from gas.

items.title: Remote residents 'cooking from the inside' sue NT government

children.content: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say the scorching hot outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they’re suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

image.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.

items.title: 500,000 Australians wait for end to 'limbo' as Butler readies NDIS plan

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler must end the uncertainty half a million people have been living in when he announces changes to the nation's disability scheme, mental health advocates say.

articles.title: Government to pay for showers for at-home aged care recipients

children.content: The decision to scrap the plan to charge older Australians $50/hour for showers was made after months of sustained criticism, including warnings that people were forgoing other care to afford the service.

image.altText: Mark Butler standing in a corridor in a suit

articles.title: Closure of specialist centres for children with autism puts spotlight on NDIS funding

children.content: The nationwide closure of specialist centres has put a spotlight on the adequacy of National Disability Insurance Scheme funding for children with "severe, profound, lifelong" autism.

items.title: 'Back of every parent's mind': Brain experts, clubs welcome NRL changes

children.content: Junior rugby league clubs and neuroscientists have welcomed changes to address growing concerns around injuries but say more should be done to protect children.

items.title: Dispute leaves devastated home owners stuck in 'limbo'

children.content: Five years after signing contracts and paying their deposits, families say their lives have been put on hold as they wait to move into their "forever homes" in Newcastle.

items.title: Carpooling an 'effective solution' to fuel prices, so why aren't more doing it?

children.content: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

items.title: ASX set to drop, markets on edge as Trump extends ceasefire

children.content: Renewed concerns about the Middle East war have outweighed initial optimism over a round of solid corporate earnings despite President Donald Trump extending the ceasefire. Follow live.

items.title: Chocolate-box violin inspires teen to retrace relative's Anzac footsteps

children.content: Ernest Sidney Pilcher died of his wounds in France on June 25, 1918. This Anzac Day, inspired by a remarkable family relic, his great-great-great-nephew will deliver a eulogy at his gravesite.

items.title: What 100 years of Australian soil can tell us about our history and future

children.content: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

items.title: Japan to sell lethal arms in move away from post-war pacifism

children.content: Japan has overhauled its decades-long defence export rule, allowing the country to sell lethal weapons internationally amid rising global tensions.

items.title: Researchers develop surfboard fins to repel sharks after recent attacks

items.title: El Salvador holds mass trial of alleged gang members for 29,000 murders
